frameworkName: State Bank of Pakistan IT Governance Framework 2024
applicability: All licensed banks, microfinance institutions, and payment service providers operating under SBP jurisdiction in Pakistan

controls.title: Data Encryption at Rest
controls.description: All customer financial data stored in databases, file systems, or backup media must be encrypted using SBP-approved cryptographic algorithms.
requiredEvidence: Encryption algorithm and key length documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Key management policy specifying rotation schedule, HSM/KMS storage, and access controls documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Database encryption configuration referenced in SDS Database Schema section
Backup encryption standard documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Multi-Factor Authentication for System Access
controls.description: All users accessing banking systems, admin panels, and sensitive financial modules must authenticate using at least two independent authentication factors.
requiredEvidence: MFA policy and supported factor types documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authentication subsection
Authentication flow diagram in SDS Section 1.1 Architecture Diagram showing MFA enforcement points
Admin and privileged user MFA requirements in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)

controls.title: Data Transmission Encryption
controls.description: All data transmitted between system components, client applications, and external integrations must use approved transport layer security protocols.
requiredEvidence: TLS version requirements and approved cipher suites documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
API endpoint security confirming HTTPS enforcement documented in SDS API Contracts section
Certificate management policy referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Privileged Access Management
controls.description: Privileged accounts with elevated system access must be governed by a formal access management policy including just-in-time access, session recording, and periodic review.
requiredEvidence: Privileged access management policy documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authorization subsection
Role definitions for privileged accounts documented in SDS Component Specifications section
Periodic access review process referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Security Incident Response Plan
controls.description: A documented security incident response plan must exist covering detection, containment, eradication, and recovery for cyber security events with SBP breach notification timelines.
requiredEvidence: Incident response procedure with SBP breach notification timeline documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Security incident escalation path documented in SDS Security Architecture section
SOC integration or incident detection mechanism described in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Network Segmentation and Perimeter Security
controls.description: Banking system infrastructure must implement network segmentation separating production, testing, and management networks with firewall controls at each boundary.
requiredEvidence: Network segmentation architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section and Section 1.1 Architecture Diagram
Firewall and network access control requirements documented in SDS Security Architecture section
Intrusion detection requirement referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Data Residency and Localisation
controls.description: All customer financial data and personally identifiable information of Pakistani citizens must be stored on servers physically located within Pakistan.
requiredEvidence: Data residency policy documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) citing SBP IT Governance Framework 2024
Hosting provider and data centre location specified in SDS Integration Points section
Cross-border data transfer restrictions documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection

controls.title: Customer Data Classification and Handling
controls.description: All customer data must be classified by sensitivity level and handled according to defined procedures for each classification tier.
requiredEvidence: Data classification tiers and handling rules documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
PII fields identified and annotated in SDS Database Schema section
Data handling procedures referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Data Retention and Secure Disposal
controls.description: Customer financial records must be retained for the regulatory minimum period and securely disposed of upon expiry per an approved retention schedule.
requiredEvidence: Data retention periods per data category documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Secure disposal mechanism described in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Archival strategy for long-term financial records referenced in SDS Database Schema section

controls.title: Data Loss Prevention Controls
controls.description: Technical and procedural controls must prevent unauthorised exfiltration of customer financial data through email, removable media, or unauthorised network channels.
requiredEvidence: DLP controls documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Network egress controls described in SDS Integration Points section
Data transfer restrictions referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Customer Consent and Privacy Management
controls.description: Customer consent for data collection and processing must be obtained, recorded, and managed with mechanisms allowing consent withdrawal.
requiredEvidence: Customer consent requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Consent management component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Privacy notice and consent UI described in SRS functional requirements with testable acceptance criteria

controls.title: System Availability and Business Continuity
controls.description: Core banking systems must maintain defined uptime SLAs and have tested business continuity plans covering failure scenarios and recovery objectives.
requiredEvidence: Availability SLA and RTO/RPO targets documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) with specific banking transaction benchmarks
High availability and failover architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section
Disaster recovery strategy referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Change Management and Release Control
controls.description: All changes to production banking systems must follow a formal change management process including impact assessment, approval, and rollback procedures.
requiredEvidence: Change management process referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Deployment pipeline and approval gates described in SDS Component Specifications section
Rollback procedure documented in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Patch Management and Vulnerability Remediation
controls.description: Security patches for operating systems, middleware, and application frameworks must be applied within defined timeframes based on vulnerability severity.
requiredEvidence: Patch management policy with severity-based timelines documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Technology stack with version management referenced in SDS Component Specifications section
Vulnerability scanning requirement documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Capacity Planning and Performance Management
controls.description: Banking systems must undergo regular capacity planning to ensure performance SLAs are met under peak load conditions including month-end processing spikes.
requiredEvidence: Performance and scalability requirements with banking transaction volume benchmarks documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Scalability and auto-scaling architecture described in SDS Architecture Overview section
Load testing requirements referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Secure Software Development Lifecycle
controls.description: All banking software must be developed following a secure SDLC including security requirements, code review, static analysis, and security testing.
requiredEvidence: Secure SDLC requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) referencing OWASP Top 10
Security testing requirement documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Code review and SAST integration referenced in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: IT Governance and Accountability Framework
controls.description: A formal IT governance structure must be established with defined roles, responsibilities, and accountability for technology decisions and risk management.
requiredEvidence: IT governance roles and responsibilities documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
System ownership and accountability structure referenced in project scope documentation

controls.title: Third-Party and Vendor Risk Management
controls.description: All third-party technology vendors and outsourced service providers must be assessed for security and operational risk before engagement and monitored continuously.
requiredEvidence: Third-party vendor risk assessment requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
External integrations and vendor dependencies listed in SDS Integration Points section
Vendor security requirements referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Regulatory Reporting and SBP Returns
controls.description: Systems collecting or processing regulatory data must support accurate and timely submission of SBP-mandated returns and reports.
requiredEvidence: Regulatory reporting requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with specific SBP return references
SBP reporting API or data portal integration documented in SDS Integration Points section
AML and suspicious activity reporting mechanism described in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Fraud Detection and Prevention Controls
controls.description: Real-time fraud detection mechanisms must be implemented for all financial transaction processing with configurable rule engines and alert thresholds.
requiredEvidence: Fraud detection requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with specific transaction monitoring rules
Fraud detection component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Alert and escalation mechanism documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Anti-Money Laundering Compliance
controls.description: Systems processing financial transactions must implement AML screening controls including customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, and sanctions list checking.
requiredEvidence: AML and KYC requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) referencing SBP AML/CFT Regulations
AML screening component and sanctions list integration described in SDS Component Specifications section
PEP and sanctions screening API documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Audit Trail and Immutable Logging
controls.description: All financial transactions, system access events, and configuration changes must be logged to an immutable audit trail accessible for regulatory inspection.
requiredEvidence: Audit logging requirements with immutability and retention period documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Audit logging component described in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection
SIEM or log aggregation integration referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Cybersecurity Risk Assessment
controls.description: A formal cybersecurity risk assessment must be conducted before system go-live and periodically thereafter covering threat modelling and risk treatment plans.
requiredEvidence: Cybersecurity risk assessment requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Threat model and key risk areas described in SDS Security Architecture section
Penetration testing and VAPT requirement referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Role-Based Access Control and Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Access to banking system functions must be governed by role-based access control with segregation of duties enforced for sensitive financial operations.
requiredEvidence: RBAC model and role definitions documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authorization subsection
Maker-checker or dual-approval workflow described in SDS Component Specifications section
Segregation of duties requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)

controls.title: IT Asset Management and Inventory
controls.description: A complete and current inventory of all IT assets including hardware, software, and cloud resources must be maintained and reviewed periodically.
requiredEvidence: Technology stack and component inventory documented in SDS Component Specifications section
Third-party libraries and dependencies listed in SDS Component Specifications section with version information
Cloud resource inventory referenced in SDS Integration Points section
